Object.Name: Zigbee Coordinator
Object.Description1: Zigbee network coordinator management. Network formation, device joining, EZSP/Z-Stack support, and mesh orchestration.

Item.Name: Network State
Item.Description: Current state of the Zigbee network: 0=Offline (coordinator not operational), 1=Forming (creating new network), 2=Joined (joined existing network), 3=Ready (network operational, accepting devices), 4=Leaving (shutting down network), 5=Error (error state requiring attention).

Item.Name: PAN ID
Item.Description: 16-bit Personal Area Network identifier (0x0000-0xFFFF). Uniquely identifies the Zigbee network. Default 0x1A62. Must be unique within radio range to avoid conflicts with neighboring networks.

Item.Name: Extended PAN ID
Item.Description: 64-bit extended PAN identifier in hexadecimal format (16 characters). Provides globally unique network identifier. Used for network selection and joining process. Must be configured before network formation.

Item.Name: Channel
Item.Description: 802.15.4 radio channel number (11-26 for 2.4GHz band). Channels 11, 15, 20, 25 recommended for minimal Wi-Fi interference. Select based on site survey to avoid congestion. Cannot be changed while network is active.

Item.Name: Network Key
Item.Description: 128-bit AES encryption key for network security (16 bytes). Write-only for security. Should be randomly generated for production deployments. Used to encrypt all network traffic. Changing key requires network reformation.

Item.Name: Permit Join Status
Item.Description: Current permit join status. True when network is accepting new devices, false when closed. Automatically set to false when permit join duration expires. Query this resource to check if joining is currently enabled.

Item.Name: Device Count
Item.Description: Total number of devices currently joined to the network (excluding coordinator). Updates automatically when devices join or leave. Maximum depends on coordinator hardware capabilities (typically 100-200 devices).

Item.Name: Coordinator IEEE Address
Item.Description: 64-bit IEEE MAC address of coordinator in hexadecimal format (16 characters). Globally unique identifier burned into coordinator hardware. Used for network security and device binding operations.

Item.Name: Coordinator Type
Item.Description: Type of Zigbee coordinator adapter: 0=Unknown, 1=EZSP (Silicon Labs EmberZNet - EFR32), 2=Z-Stack (Texas Instruments - CC2531/CC2652), 3=ConBee (Dresden Elektronik), 4=ZiGate. Detected automatically during initialization.

Item.Name: Firmware Version
Item.Description: Coordinator firmware version string. Reports protocol stack version (e.g., "EZSP v8", "Z-Stack 3.0"). Used for compatibility checking and troubleshooting. Update firmware through coordinator-specific tools.

Item.Name: TX Power
Item.Description: Transmit power in dBm (-20 to +20). Higher values increase range but power consumption. Default 20 dBm. Regional regulations may limit maximum power. Adjust based on deployment environment and regulatory requirements.

Item.Name: Security Level
Item.Description: Network security level (0-7). 5 (standard security with encryption and authentication) is default and recommended for production. Level 0 disables security (testing only). Higher levels may impact performance.

Item.Name: Serial Port
Item.Description: Serial device path for coordinator communication (e.g., "/dev/ttyUSB0", "/dev/ttyACM0"). Must have read/write permissions. Verify path before network formation. Requires restart if changed.

Item.Name: Baud Rate
Item.Description: Serial communication baud rate (typically 115200 for EZSP, 115200 for Z-Stack). Must match coordinator configuration. Common values: 9600, 19200, 38400, 57600, 115200. Incorrect baud rate prevents communication.

Item.Name: Route Table Size
Item.Description: Number of entries in coordinator route table. Shows active routing paths to devices in mesh network. Larger networks require more route table entries. Monitor for network health assessment.

Item.Name: Neighbor Table Size
Item.Description: Number of entries in coordinator neighbor table. Lists directly reachable devices (one-hop neighbors). Important for mesh topology visualization and network planning. High values indicate good coordinator placement.

Item.Name: Form Network
Item.Description: Execute to form new Zigbee network with current configuration (PAN ID, channel, extended PAN ID). Takes 3-10 seconds. Network must be offline. All devices will need to rejoin if network parameters change.

Item.Name: Leave Network
Item.Description: Execute to leave current network and shut down coordinator. All devices will lose connectivity. Use before changing network configuration. Network can be reformed with same or different parameters after leaving.

Item.Name: Permit Join Command
Item.Description: Execute to enable/disable device joining. Argument: duration in seconds (0=disable, 1-254=duration, 255=forever). Recommended: 60-180 seconds for controlled joining. Security risk if left open indefinitely.

Item.Name: Start Touchlink
Item.Description: Execute to initiate Zigbee Touchlink commissioning (ZLL). Used for adding devices without requiring traditional pairing mode. Device must be within close proximity. Useful for light bulbs and remote controls.

Item.Name: Backup Network
Item.Description: Execute to backup current network configuration including keys, PAN ID, and device bindings. Enables network restoration after coordinator replacement or failure. Store backup securely due to security key inclusion.
